en.name: Mean arterial pressure
en.shortDescription: Mean arterial pressure (MAP) — the average arterial pressure over one cardiac cycle; the key perfusion-pressure target in critical care. Any site, method or position.
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Mean arterial pressure (MAP) is the average pressure in the arteries over one complete cardiac cycle. It is not the simple average of systolic and diastolic values: invasive monitors compute it as the area under the arterial pressure curve, and non-invasive monitors estimate it (approximately diastolic + one-third of the pulse pressure). Measuring it produces a numeric pressure value in millimetres of mercury (mmHg), obtained either invasively (arterial line and transducer) or non-invasively (automated cuff) [1]. MAP is the principal blood-pressure target in critical care because it best represents the pressure driving blood into the organs; ICU protocols are typically written around a MAP goal (commonly ≥ 65 mmHg). This concept set captures **numeric mean arterial pressure values** across all measurement sites, methods, body positions and respiratory phases. Systolic and diastolic pressures are covered by dedicated concept sets, as is central venous pressure. ## Included Concepts This concept set contains 23 standard concepts (20 LOINC + 3 SNOMED) organised below by what the source documents about the measurement. A concept may appear in more than one group. The default for an unqualified MAP value is *3027598 / 8478-0 — Mean blood pressure (LOINC)* or its SNOMED equivalents. ### Generic mean pressure - *3027598 / 8478-0 — Mean blood pressure (LOINC)*: The generic mean blood pressure, no site/method/position specified. Default mapping target. - *4239021 / 6797001 — Mean blood pressure (SNOMED)*: SNOMED equivalent of the generic mean blood pressure. - *37168599 / 1285244000 — Mean arterial pressure (SNOMED)*: SNOMED concept explicitly named "mean arterial pressure"; equivalent in meaning to the generic mean blood pressure and the closest match to how clinicians refer to MAP. ### By measurement method - *21492241 / 76536-2 — Mean blood pressure by Noninvasive (LOINC)*: MAP measured non-invasively, method stated. - *4108289 / 251074006 — Non-invasive mean arterial pressure (SNOMED)*: SNOMED equivalent for a non-invasively measured MAP. - *21490852 / 76214-6 — Invasive Mean blood pressure (LOINC)*: MAP derived from an invasive arterial line. Standard in the ICU and operating room. - *4108290 / 251075007 — Invasive mean arterial pressure (SNOMED)*: SNOMED equivalent for an invasively measured MAP. - *21490778 / 75996-9 — Mean blood pressure by Continuous non-invasive monitoring (LOINC)*: MAP from continuous non-invasive monitoring (e.g., finger-cuff volume-clamp devices). ### By measurement site - *3005468 / 8506-8 — Radial artery Mean blood pressure (LOINC)*: MAP at the radial artery (usual arterial-line site). - *3015847 / 8543-1 — Radial artery - right Mean blood pressure (LOINC)* and *3009075 / 8542-3 — Radial artery - left Mean blood pressure (LOINC)*: Side-specified radial measurements. - *3019870 / 8504-3 — Femoral artery Mean blood pressure (LOINC)*: MAP at the femoral artery. - *3018701 / 8535-7 — Femoral artery - right Mean blood pressure (LOINC)* and *3012895 / 8534-0 — Femoral artery - left Mean blood pressure (LOINC)*: Side-specified femoral measurements. - *3015939 / 8418-6 — Renal artery - right Mean blood pressure (LOINC)* and *3010443 / 8417-8 — Renal artery - left Mean blood pressure (LOINC)*: MAP measured in a renal artery (specialised, e.g., during renal angiography). ### By patient position Use only when the source explicitly records body position; otherwise use the generic concept. - *3018336 / 8458-2 — Mean blood pressure--supine (LOINC)*: Measured lying flat. - *3012526 / 8456-6 — Mean blood pressure--sitting (LOINC)*: Measured sitting. - *3017490 / 8457-4 — Mean blood pressure--standing (LOINC)*: Measured standing. ### By respiratory phase - *3013032 / 8449-1 — Mean blood pressure--inspiration (LOINC)* and *3022152 / 8448-3 — Mean blood pressure--expiration (LOINC)*: Measured at a specified phase of breathing. ### By clinical condition - *36031915 / 95822-3 — Mean blood pressure--during anesthesia (LOINC)*: MAP measured under anaesthesia. - *36031385 / 95821-5 — Mean blood pressure by Noninvasive--during anesthesia (LOINC)*: Non-invasive MAP measured under anaesthesia (method + condition). ## Excluded Concepts The set is a curated list of numeric mean arterial pressure measurements. Out-of-scope concepts from the surrounding blood-pressure hierarchies are intentionally not included: ambulatory/24-hour summaries, percentile values, maximum/minimum/target derived values, self-reported pressures, and any non-numeric findings. Systolic, diastolic and central venous pressures are captured by their own dedicated concept sets. ## Mapping Notes - *Default concept*: When the source records a MAP with no site, method or position, map to Mean blood pressure (3027598 / 8478-0, LOINC), or its SNOMED equivalent Mean arterial pressure (37168599 / 1285244000) if the source is SNOMED-aligned. Do not infer an undocumented method or site. - *Invasive vs. non-invasive*: Map to the invasive concept (21490852 / 76214-6, LOINC) only when the source identifies an arterial line, and to the non-invasive concept (21492241 / 76536-2, LOINC) only when a non-invasive method is stated. Otherwise use the generic concept. - *MAP from a monitor*: Bedside monitors usually display MAP alongside systolic/diastolic; an unlabelled MAP value maps to Mean blood pressure (3027598 / 8478-0, LOINC). - *Common source labels*: "MAP", "MBP", "PAM" (pression artérielle moyenne), "Mean BP", "NIBP mean" → Mean blood pressure (3027598 / 8478-0, LOINC). "ABP mean", "Art MAP" (arterial line) → Invasive Mean blood pressure (21490852 / 76214-6, LOINC). - *Units*: Mean arterial pressure is reported in mmHg. ## References [1] Magder S. The meaning of blood pressure. Crit Care. 2018;22(1):257. Available: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6172730/

fr.name: Pression artérielle moyenne
fr.shortDescription: Pression artérielle moyenne (PAM) — la pression artérielle moyenne sur un cycle cardiaque ; la cible de pression de perfusion clé en réanimation. Tout site, méthode ou position.
